description.en: Widely grown evergreen fruit tree across Sri Lanka's low- and mid-country Wet and Intermediate zones. Sweet brown-fleshed fruit with translucent jelly-like flesh. In Sri Lanka the native *mee* tree (*Madhuca longifolia* syn. *Bassia longifolia*) is commonly used as the rootstock for sapodilla grafting — distinctive to Sri Lankan sapodilla practice. Sap (chicle) was historically used for chewing gum production.

scientific_name.quote: The sapathilla tree, which is also known as rata mi, bears the scientific name of Manilkara zapota

description.quote: in Sri Lanka, mee (Bassia longifolia) is commonly used as a rootstock
